strategic_inferences.rational_explanation: Brent at $110 is a direct consequence of physical supply disruption. The market is pricing genuine scarcity risk from the Hormuz situation. This is not strategic manipulation — it is supply-demand mechanics operating on a commodity with inelastic short-term demand.
strategic_inferences.alternative_explanation: Speculative premium has pushed oil above fundamental value due to media framing ('Iran War'), and actual physical disruption is less severe than pricing suggests.

If significant institutional settlement were occurring on XRPL — even through non-DEX channels — we would expect secondary indicators (higher transaction counts, higher fee burns, more RLUSD velocity) to reflect it. ODL volume ceased publication precisely because the data was there but Ripple chose not to share it.

structural_gaps.description: ODL volume data not publicly available. Ripple ceased publication. No independent measurement methodology exists.
structural_gaps.gap_reason: Ripple is the sole entity with comprehensive ODL data and has chosen not to share it. Third-party on-chain analytics cannot reliably distinguish ODL flows from other XRP transactions.
structural_gaps.promotable_if: Ripple resumes publication, OR an independent third party develops a verified ODL measurement methodology, OR Ripple Payments on-chain signatures become identifiable.

structural_gaps.description: Token velocity / average hold times not measurable.

structural_gaps.promotable_if: An on-chain analytics provider develops XRP-specific velocity metrics, OR XRPL native analytics tools provide this data.

structural_gaps.description: Japan/SBI institutional activity not observable through current intelligence channels.

structural_gaps.description: XRPL Payment transaction volumes (non-DEX direct payments) at institutional scale not currently distinguished from DEX volume in the monitoring pipeline.
